project_overview.project_name: Innovative Systems to Promote Integrated, Resilient, and Enhanced Responses to Women and Girls' Health Project (INSPIRER)
project_overview.project_development_objective: To improve women and girls' health outcomes and strengthen health systems in climate-stressed areas
project_overview.total_financing: US$70.1 million (IDA Credit: US$60 million, GFF Grant: US$10.1 million, with US$60 million financing gap)
project_overview.beneficiaries: 3.8 million pregnant women accessing antenatal care, 1 million women eligible for cervical cancer screening, 8 million girls receiving HPV vaccination annually, 38,000 HEWs receiving digital training, 12 million community members indirectly benefiting

geographic_scope.coverage_type: national with focus on climate-stressed and conflict-affected areas
geographic_scope.fragile_or_conflict_affected_areas: yes - project specifically targets climate-stressed and conflict-affected areas, with Ethiopia classified as fragile state

key_components.component_name: Expand access to quality interventions for women and girls, including through tech-enabled care
key_components.description: HPV vaccination program, HEW digital training platforms, AI-enabled ultrasound machines, m-mama emergency response system

key_components.component_name: Improve service delivery by innovative interventions in climate stressed & conflict affected areas
key_components.description: Solar energy for health centers, climate-resilient facility renovations, waterless toilet technologies, medical waste management systems

key_components.component_name: Strengthen data systems and reinforce decision-making to ensure high quality care
key_components.description: Health data governance standards, big data analytics for climate-health integration, institutional capacity building
Program management, impact evaluations, technical support

implementation_arrangements.coordination_mechanisms: Project Steering Committee at federal level, Project Technical Committee, coordination with Regional Health Bureaus (RHBs)
implementation_arrangements.procurement_approach: World Bank Procurement Regulations for IPF Borrowers, use of Standard Procurement Documents
implementation_arrangements.monitoring_and_evaluation: Quarterly IFRs, semi-annual progress reports, annual financial audits, mid-term and endline evaluations, impact evaluation of m-mama system

identified_risks.risk_description: Political instability and security risks in different parts of the country disrupting healthcare services and project activities
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Regular risk identification at milestones, tailored contingency plans, leveraging portfolio-wide risk management strategies

identified_risks.risk_description: Exchange rate liberalization at early stage, significant financing needs, potential forex access issues for pharmaceutical imports
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Government implementing macroeconomic reforms with World Bank DPO and IMF support, prioritizing pro-poor spending, requesting debt relief under G20 Common Framework

identified_risks.risk_description: Limited capacity to implement innovative interventions and digital technologies, human resource constraints, weak data systems
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Targeted training, technical assistance, capacity building initiatives incorporated in project design

identified_risks.risk_description: Security implications, FM staffing challenges, multiple implementing entities, internal control weaknesses, procurement delays
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Standard operating procedures for security situations, qualified staff recruitment, stringent internal controls, close World Bank supervision

identified_risks.risk_description: Electricity access deficits, low internet penetration in rural areas, limited digital literacy, data security concerns
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Use of WoredaNet and offline networks, solar energy support, digital literacy training for HEWs, data privacy guidelines

triggered_safeguard_policies: ESS1: Assessment and Management of Environmental and Social Risks
ESS3: Resource Efficiency and Pollution Prevention
ESS7: Indigenous Peoples/Sub-Saharan African Historically Underserved Traditional Local Communities
safeguards_and_social.grievance_redress_mechanism: yes - to be established within 2 months of effectiveness, Community Score Card platform for citizen engagement
safeguards_and_social.gender_considerations: Project prioritizes gender equity, targets women and girls' health gaps, addresses digital skill gaps of female HEWs, includes gender-disaggregated data analysis
vulnerable_groups: women and girls in climate-stressed areas

fcv_relevant_details.conflict_sensitivity: Project specifically designed for conflict-affected areas, includes security risk mitigation measures, standard operating procedures for conflict situations
fcv_relevant_details.fragility_context: Ethiopia classified as fragile state, project addresses climate vulnerability, drought impacts, infrastructure damage from conflicts, healthcare system fragility
fcv_relevant_details.security_considerations: Security risks affecting project implementation and supervision, potential SEA/SH risks in conflict areas, safety risks to project workers in insecure regions
fcv_relevant_details.displacement_or_refugees: yes - project targets internally displaced persons and refugees as vulnerable beneficiaries, addresses health needs of displaced populations
